methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xiongyi Cui and Linxi Fan. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 9 peer companies—1 public (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms operating in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector. Top private comparables include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers, but applying filters for stage consistency, timing proximity, or comparable valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any narrowed cohort. A median derived from the full sector universe would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of robotics companies across development stages and business models. The limited depth of narrow peer data precludes reliable triangulation via this method.

Without a sufficiently populated comparable set meeting stage and tier specificity, this analysis cannot produce a defensible point estimate or range. Valuation of Sunday Robotics would require either: (1) expansion of the peer universe to adjacent frontier tech sectors with comparable risk and capital intensity profiles; (2) forward-looking cash flow methods if financial projections are available; or (3) direct market feedback from recent seed-stage financings in robotics. The current dataset supports only the observation that the target operates in a sector with established public and private reference points, but insufficient granularity to anchor a quantitative valuation.
